item.dc:creator: RoonSwanson1

item.title: Today, I Take The Role Of My Favorite Muppet, The Swedish Chef - Have Some Meatballs!
item.description:
Delicious, savory meatballs smothered in a fragrantly spiced sauce; it's a hard combination to beat. Served with your starch of choice(I went with egg noodles) and a green veggie(steamed broccoli), this is a perfect comfort meal to tuck into that can be ready in an hour's time. Come with me and I'll show you how!
This recipe is inspired by https://pinchofyum.com/swedish-meatballs . I made a few minor variations to make it my own. For the meatballs, you will need: 4 slices of white sandwich bread, crusts removed 1/2 cup(118 mL) heavy cream 1 teaspoon(3.5 g) garlic powder 1 teaspoon(2.5 g) onion powder 1 lb.(454 g) ground beef 1 lb.(454 g) ground pork 1 large egg 2 tablespoons(30 g) brown sugar 2 teaspoons(12 g) salt 1 teaspoon(5 g) MSG Black pepper to taste For the sauce, you will need: 2 tablespoons(28 g) butter 2 tablespoons(15-16 g) all-purpose flour 1 1/2 cups(354 mL) beef broth (more as needed) 1 cup(236 mL) heavy cream 1 tablespoon(15 mL) Worcestershire sauce (more to taste) A few dashes of aromatic bitters 1–2 teaspoons(5-10 g) brown sugar Salt to taste Black pepper to taste 1 teaspoon(5 g) MSG Pinch of nutmeg Pinch of ground ginger Pinch of allspice
Here we begin making the meatballs. We want our oven to be 400 F so we will preheat it now. Place the bread in a large mixing bowl and pour over the 1/2 cup cream. Manipulate the bread so that it soaks up most of the cream. Let sit 5-10 minutes.
Add the remaining meatball ingredients to the bowl. Get dirty with your bad self! Use your hands to make a fairly homogenous mixture, as you would for a meatloaf. Make sure to break up any large pieces of bread.
The final mixture should be a vibrant pink color and should be slightly wet and a little tacky.
Roll the mixture into roughly even balls and place them in a roasting pan, casserole, or baking sheet. I like large meatballs, so I rolled them about 2.5"-3". You can, of course, roll more smaller meatballs; you just need to cook them for less time. Place the pan in the pre-heated oven for approximately 40 minutes for meatballs this size; if you are working with meatballs 1"-1.5" in diameter, you may be able to cook them in 25-30 minutes. The best metric is a meat thermometer reading 170 F when inserted in the center of the largest meatball.
While the meatballs bake, start a pot of water boiling for noodles(or whatever starch you like). In a large skillet or Dutch oven, melt the butter for the sauce over high heat.
When the butter is completely melted, evenly coat it with the flour. Stir constantly until the mixture becomes pasty and takes on a tan color, 2-3 minutes.
Add the beef stock to the roux(pronounced roo; it's that pasty flour mixture you just made). Reduce the heat to medium. Stir constantly until the mixture is evenly incorporated.
When the mixture begins to thicken and bubble, add the cream.
Stir until the mixture is homogenous. BTW, you really should scrape any browned bits off the bottom of the pan(called a fond) and incorporate them into the sauce - they are guaranteed flavor boosters.
Add your brown sugar, salt, pepper, nutmeg, ginger, allspice, W sauce, aromatic bitter, and MSG. Stir to combine well.
Taste your sauce, and if you want black pepper, don't be like me - do it with the rest of the seasonings. Adjust salt and pepper to your liking. If your sauce is thicker than you like, at any point, simply add a little extra beef stock, stir well, and taste, then adjust for seasoning.
When the meatballs are done baking, place them in the sauce.
Pour in the fat and drippings from your baking vessel.
Stir the mixture and thoroughly coat all the meatballs.
Reduce to low heat and cover, stirring occasionally, until your starch and veggies are ready.
Here I have buttered steamed broccoli and buttered egg noodles. If I have to tell you how to steam broccoli and boil egg noodles, let alone how to butter each, you aren't ready to make this meal. Git gud.
Here is a pot full of deliciousness right before your very eyes.
The noodles, dripping with sauce, served with two hefty meatballs and a wonderful side of broccoli, make the meal. I'm sure that you will find this an extravagant treat. First yu put di chickee - err... I mean, cook on!
item.dc:creator: laotzu42

item.title: A favorite for my 7th cake day
item.description: I love all nerds and especially nerdy women. Smart, funny, possibly fluent in Klingon. With well established D&D characters and closets full of cosplay. Science and math jokes are my favorite which is why this video really got me. Thanks to this wonderful community for keeping me entertained, informed, and relatively sane through this alternative timeline we must have been living in for the past 7+ years. It could have been higgs-boson collider, could have been butthole-bot. Something ripped a hole in our timeline. Maybe if we restored the butthole-bot it will fix it? Just putting that out there.
item.dc:creator: iggylights

item.title: Sad news regarding the UK drag scene.
item.description: I've only just gotten into watching Drag and RuPaul's, too ??. I lost my uncle before Christmas, too. No death is nice, but I hope she wasn't in pain or suffering. I hope it wasn't horrible cancer like what my uncle had.

item.title: Memes vol 461
item.description:
Me
Ok here are the 9 other calico critters tax
And I got a new crochet possum. I love her, her name is Luanne
Big Mama tax. Don’t let her looks fool you, she actually adores my boyfriend- she’s just very angry looking. But she jumped up there all her own and was watching him play video games
Scruffy and Nessie tax
Froggy and Liza Jane tax

item.title: Finally, hope for Republicans in this new year!
item.description: Scientists were apparently able to grow a human spine in vitro, so there may be hope yet for the US as we move in to a Republican controlled White House, Congress, and Supreme Republican Court of the United States (SCROTUS). Sauce: https://www.popularmechanics.com/science/health/a63241663/scientists-grow-human-spine/

item.title: Blizzard Weekend Meme Dump
item.description:
This feels relevant having to shop for groceries before a winter storm hit us! We supposedly got 15” of snow, but we haven’t been out to measure yet.
I just cannot do it. I also did not grow up doing LAN parties, I just played games on my own. That’s probably part of it.
Get ready for some Ratatouille memes, you’ll find there’s a theme occurring for the next few.
I literally birthed a child in 2019 and I still react like this.
I can have more than one goal.
(This is relevant because my husband is named Cody) (it might not be as funny to other people lol)
I love meat and cheese on a piece of wood.
For anyone wondering, my friend group has decided who is who here. I am the mantis.
Both is good.
item.dc:creator: clevergrrl420

item.title: These foxes playing in the snow outside Magdalen College at Oxford University
item.description: Via Eleonora Svanberg on X.
item.dc:creator: Newitt

item.title: Etch A Sketch art show recap!
item.description: A number of you have asked for an update on the Etch A Sketch art show in Las Vegas and tonight I am finally delivering! Better late than never amirite? This was such a gosh darn neat experience and I'm so grateful to be part of this unique and silly community. I'm also so honored to have met a few of you who caught my first video about the opening here on Imgur! :) If you happen to find yourself in the Las Vegas area within the next week, it would mean the world to me if you considered checking the show out! https://thelibrarydistrict.org/blogs/post/unshakeable-etch-a-sketch-art-around-the-world-1986-2024/
item.dc:creator: pikajane
